dataset: Fiji ImageJ built-in Fluorescent Cells sample

pi_items.source: task.json and task description explicitly require 'at least 5 distinct nuclei'; verifier.py confirms row_count >= 5
verifier.py uses this as validation threshold, but no source documentation found confirming this is the expected range for the Fluorescent Cells sample image
verifier.py uses this as validation threshold, but no source documentation found confirming this is the expected range for the Fluorescent Cells sample image

Web search confirmed that in 8-bit images (0-255), typical maximum intensity should use 50-75% of range. Value of 50 is reasonable threshold for signal/background distinction in fluorescence microscopy.
Standard maximum value for 8-bit grayscale images, which are the standard format for fluorescence microscopy. Web search confirmed 8-bit range is 0-255.
setup_task.sh creates RESULTS_DIR at $DATA_DIR/results (where DATA_DIR=/home/ga/ImageJ_Data), and export_result.sh reads from this path

privileged_info_summary: The task measures Full Width at Half Maximum (FWHM) on cell nuclei in the Fiji ImageJ built-in Fluorescent Cells sample image. The task requires measuring at least 5 distinct nuclei using line profiles and recording peak intensity (range 50-255), background intensity, and FWHM values. The output file is saved to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/fwhm_measurements.csv. The Fluorescent Cells sample is a real (non-synthetic) multi-channel fluorescence microscopy image available as a standard built-in sample in Fiji/ImageJ accessed via File > Open Samples > Fluorescent Cells.
